heading: Necessary expenditures
label: 522 Necessary expenditures
text: The Director of the Office of Management and Budget may make necessary expenditures for the Office under regulations prescribed by the President.
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevised SectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)52231:17(a)(related to expenses).(related to expenses), ; Reorg. Plan No. 1 of 1939, eff. , § 1, ; Reorg. Plan No. 2 of 1970, eff. , § 102(a), .June 10, 1921, ch. 18, § 208(a)42 Stat. 2253 Stat. 142384 Stat. 2085July 1, 1939July 1, 1970The words “for rent in the District of Columbia, printing, binding, telegrams, telephone service, law books, books of reference, periodicals, stationery, furniture, office equipment, other supplies, and” are omitted as covered by titles 5, 40, and 44, and as being included in “necessary expenditures”. The words “within the appropriations made therefor” are omitted as unnecessary.
